headline: Send your body a message of safety
subtext: Calm self-talk that tells an over-protective brain the alarm can stand down.
steps: Notice the sensation without bracing against it.
Tell yourself, gently and truthfully, that you are safe.
Let the breath slow as the alarm settles.
caption:
A symptom is not always a warning. Often it is a false alarm from an over-protective nervous system.

Messages of safety are a simple way to answer that alarm. When a familiar sensation appears, you meet it with calm rather than fear, and remind yourself, truthfully, that you are safe. Over time this lowers the threat the brain attaches to the feeling, and the signal quietens.

In chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, fear is fuel. Teaching the brain safety, moment by moment, is part of how recovery begins.

Learn this with a practitioner at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Sending messages of safety is a core technique in pain reprocessing approaches, which help the brain reclassify safe sensations as non-threatening. Find guided practices at https://neurowaze.health
